title: BEM-914 worker review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM914_WORKER_SYSTEM_IMPROVEMENT_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-914 WORKER SYSTEM IMPROVEMENT PROTOCOL REVIEW
Status: WORKER_REVIEW_READY
Decision: APPROVED_WITH_NOTES
Findings:
- The improvement protocol is necessary and valid after Claude CONDITIONAL PASS.
- BEM-907..BEM-913 reduced the gaps but did not fully eliminate them. The next protocol must distinguish completed remediation from remaining operationalization work.
- The highest priority is independent proof: CI/Git proof must become release gate, because commit_sha null remains a real audit weakness.
- The second priority is a real dispatch lifecycle: queued -> delivered -> processing -> result -> proof -> completed/operator_gate, with deterministic consumer and no stale queued records.
- The third priority is object execution: GD, Director and Worker now have prompts/runners, but full agent behavior requires an explicit operator-approved execution model.
- The fourth priority is architecture canon: every release must update plan/fact/gap and readiness state.
- The fifth priority is pilot readiness: all synthetic preparation can continue autonomously; real pilot selection remains operator gate.
Autonomous scope:
- Implement deterministic scripts, validators, workflows using workflow_dispatch, state files, proof packages, dashboards, report packaging, and re-audit bundles.
Operator gate:
- Always-on daemon, schedule triggers, paid API, secrets, issue #31 writes, real pilot/client/scope selection, and LLM-agent runtime model.
Recommendation:
- Approve protocol with notes.
- Status should be PROTOCOL_APPROVED_WITH_OPERATOR_GATES.
No issue comments.
